irremote_library_config.message0: IR library config buffer length %1 enable universal decode %2 keep special protocols %3
irremote_library_config.tooltip: Configure RAW_BUFFER_LENGTH and whether to retain universal/special protocol parsing via compile-time macros

irremote_receiver_begin.message0: initialize IR receiver pin %1 LED feedback %2
irremote_receiver_begin.tooltip: Initialize the IR receiver; enabling LED feedback makes the onboard LED blink when receiving signals for easier debugging

irremote_sender_begin.tooltip: Initialize the IR transmitter and set the transmit pin

irremote_on_receive.tooltip: Execute inner code when an IR signal is received; check ignore repeat to prevent multiple triggers from one button press

irremote_receiver_available.tooltip: Check whether new IR data is available

irremote_get_value.tooltip: Read the specified field from received IR data

irremote_get_protocol.tooltip: Return the name or enum value of the received IR protocol

irremote_check_flag.tooltip: Check the flag status of received IR data

irremote_is_preset_key.tooltip: Match a preset key on the Arduino kit NEC mini remote

irremote_get_preset_name.tooltip: Return the name of the preset remote key; returns empty string if no match

irremote_resume.message0: IR receiver prepare for next frame
irremote_resume.tooltip: Manually prepare for the next reception; only needed when not using the "when IR received" block

irremote_send_command.message0: IR send protocol %1 address %2 command %3 repeats %4
irremote_send_command.tooltip: Send an IR signal using the specified protocol with configurable address, command, and repeat count

irremote_print_result.tooltip: Print IR receive results to serial; Summary shows protocol/address/command, Send Code shows how to replay the signal

irremote_command_equals.tooltip: Check if the received IR command value equals a specified value; works with any remote

irremote_send_nec.message0: IR send NEC address %1 command %2 repeats %3
irremote_send_nec.tooltip: Send an IR signal using NEC protocol, the most commonly used IR protocol

irremote_send_raw.tooltip: Send raw IR timing data for replaying signals of unknown protocols; data format is comma-separated microsecond values

irremote_send_pronto.tooltip: Send an IR signal in Pronto format; Pronto codes can be found in online IR code databases
